item.title: Uber to open 2 campuses in India to support product development, operations
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Uber plans to open two new engineering campuses with a capacity of nearly 10,000 people in India by the end of 2027.

item.title: Who decides what AI tells you? Campbell Brown, once Meta’s news chief, has thoughts
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: "The conversation is sort of happening in Silicon Valley around one thing, and a totally different conversation is happening among consumers."

item.title: Clio’s $500M milestone arrives just as Anthropic ups the ante
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Legal tech startups, including Clio, which just hit $500 million in ARR, are seeing massive customer adoption.

item.title: Notion just turned its workspace into a hub for AI agents
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Notion’s new developer platform lets teams connect AI agents, external data sources, and custom code directly into their workspace as the company pushes deeper into agentic productivity software.

item.title: Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Gas turbines at xAI's Colossus 2 data center have drawn a lawsuit over the company's use of "mobile" gas turbines as power plants.

item.title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The head of product for Claude Code and Cowork says that the next big step for AI is proactivity.

item.title: This is what some of the world’s largest banks of malware look like stacked as hard drives
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: What would some of the world's largest repositories of malware look like if they were stacked as hard drives, one on top of the other?

item.title: Geothermal startup Fervo Energy pops 33% in IPO debut fueled by AI data center demand
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Fervo Energy's IPO was upsized several times after potential investors asked why the enhanced geothermal startup wasn't raising more money.

item.title: X launches a History tab for bookmarks, likes, videos, and articles
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X’s new History tab combines bookmarks, likes, watched videos, and read articles into a single place, expanding the app’s role as a save-it-for-later tool.

item.title: Instagram’s new ‘Instants’ feature combines elements from Snapchat and BeReal
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The feature lets users share disappearing photos with their close friends or mutual followers that can be viewed only once and remain available for 24 hours.

item.title: Rivian spinoff Mind Robotics raises another $400M
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: Mind Robotics, which was first revealed in late 2025, has now raised more than $1 billion to date.

item.title: Who trusts Sam Altman?
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: "I believe I am an honest and trustworthy businessperson," Altman testified in federal court.

item.title: Origin Lab raises $8M to help video game companies sell data to world-model builders
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Origin Lab will serve as a marketplace where AI labs can buy high-quality licensed data, and video-game companies can sell it.

item.title: Anthropic courts a new kind of customer: small business owners
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: For founders and investors, Anthropic's new offering signals that the AI platform wars are expanding downmarket and that the next major battleground for user acquisition isn't the Fortune 500; it's the 36 million small businesses that make up the backbone of the U.S. economy.

item.title: Ransomware hackers claim breach at Foxconn, a major electronics manufacturer for Apple, Google, and Nvidia
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: A ransomware group has claimed responsibility for hacking the electronics manufacturing giant Foxconn and is attempting to extort the company.

item.title: Amazon launches an AI shopping assistant for the search bar, powered by Alexa+
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Alexa for Shopping offers a voice- and touch-enabled shopping experience across mobile, desktop, and Echo Show smart displays. Alexa for Shopping provides more personalized recommendations and automates the shopping experience across Amazon and other online retailers.

item.title: Anduril raises $5B, doubles valuation to $61B
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: After achieving $2.2 billion in revenue in 2025, the defense tech startup has raised another massive round, led by Thrive and a16z, it says.

item.title: r/WallStreetBets really hates the SEC’s proposal to weaken quarterly reporting
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The retail trading subreddit submitted the sharpest criticism yet of the financial regulator's idea of letting companies report twice per year.

item.title: US lawmakers demand answers from Instructure after Canvas data breaches
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: U.S. House lawmakers want to know how hackers broke into education tech giant Instructure twice and stole reams of data from students who use the company's flagship student data software Canvas.

item.title: Anthropic now has more business customers than OpenAI, according to Ramp data
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: A survey compiled from fintech firm Ramp’s clients’ expense data shows 34.4% of participating businesses are paying for Anthropic services, more than any other AI lab, while only 32.3% pay for OpenAI.
